SEARCH
Package core

Class ServerMonitor

java.lang.Object
core.ServerMonitor
All Implemented Interfaces:
Runnable
public class ServerMonitor extends Object implements Runnable
Monitors the health and performance of servers in the LoadBalancer. This class runs as a background thread, continuously updating server metrics and checking for threshold violations. If a server's CPU, memory, or disk usage exceeds 90%, an alert is triggered and logged.

Features:

- Runs independently in a loop, monitoring servers every 5 seconds. - Fetches cloud-based metrics if available (e.g., AWS CloudWatch). - Applies random metric fluctuations for on-premise servers. - Triggers alerts if server usage surpasses the defined threshold. - Logs alerts and updates server health status.

Usage:


 LoadBalancer balancer = new LoadBalancer();
 ServerMonitor monitor = new ServerMonitor(balancer);
 Thread monitorThread = new Thread(monitor);
 monitorThread.start();

Logging:

- Server monitor startup: `"=== SERVER MONITOR ONLINE—WATCHIN’ THE CREW ==="` - Alert if a server exceeds threshold: `"ALERT: Server X is HOT!"` - Server monitor shutdown: `"=== SERVER MONITOR OUT—PEACE ==="`

Threading:

- This class implements Runnable to run as a separate monitoring thread. - Uses synchronized (balancer) to ensure thread safety when modifying server states. - Sleeps for 5 seconds between monitoring cycles.

    Description
    ServerMonitor(LoadBalancer balancer)

  • Constructor Details

    • ServerMonitor

      public ServerMonitor(LoadBalancer balancer)
      Constructs a ServerMonitor instance tied to a LoadBalancer.
      Parameters:
      balancer - The load balancer whose servers will be monitored.

  • Method Details

    • stop

      public void stop()
      Stops the monitoring loop, allowing the thread to terminate gracefully.

      This method sets the running flag to false, signaling the monitoring loop to exit on the next iteration.

    • run

      public void run()
      Executes the server monitoring loop.

      Process:

      - Iterates through all servers managed by LoadBalancer. - If cloud integration is available, updates metrics from AWS CloudWatch. - Otherwise, applies random metric fluctuations (±10%). - Triggers alerts if CPU, memory, or disk usage exceeds 90%. - Calls balancer.checkServerHealth() to update server health statuses. - Sleeps for 5 seconds between iterations.

      Error Handling:

      - If interrupted, the thread exits gracefully. - Uses synchronized (balancer) to ensure safe multi-threaded access.
      Specified by:
      run in interface Runnable
